subject: Re: Warp not seeing all .tif files in a directory
body:
I'm also attaching the worker crash files.

On Tuesday, November 5, 2019 at 3:21:28 PM UTC-5, Andres Hernandez wrote:
>
> I'm having the same issue. I had it with 1.0.6 and now with 1.0.7. The tif 
> files stop showing up in WARP and sometimes I get a crash with an error. 
> After the crashes, I have to delete the xml files and restart processing 
> from the beginning or it gets stuck at "loading particle metadata from 
> previous run." I think it is related to aws s3 sync (multipart file 
> transfers), but it has also happened with a regular sync command (only one 
> *.tif.* temp file per file).
>
> Here is the lastcrash.txt, I am also attaching a screenshot of the error.
>
> System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed pipe.
>    at System.IO.__Error.PipeNotOpen()
>    at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.CheckConnectOperationsServer()
>    at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.WaitForConnection()
>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.SendCommand(NamedSerializableObject command)
>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Dispose()
>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Finalize()
>    at System.IO.__Error.PipeNotOpen()
>    at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.CheckConnectOperationsServer()
>    at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.WaitForConnection()
>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.SendCommand(NamedSerializableObject command)
>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Dispose()
>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Finalize()
>
>
>
> On Sunday, July 15, 2018 at 3:19:59 PM UTC-4, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>
>> The XML files are only initialized once some processing results need to 
>> be stored – not upon discovery. If a file has been successfully discovered, 
>> it will show up in the UI.
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>> On Sunday, July 15, 2018 at 9:09:31 PM UTC+2, mcia...@umich.edu wrote:
>>>
>>> There could be something like this happening as the hard drive on AWS 
>>> that we were using filled up last night, so Warp might have tried to write 
>>> files that didn't exist. 
>>>
>>> I'll try splitting them to see what happens. Where is the information 
>>> stored to indicate that it has 'seen' a file? The xml files? 
>>>
>>> Mike
>>>
>>> On Sunday, July 15, 2018 at 3:07:30 PM UTC-4, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>>>
>>>> Hi Mike,
>>>>
>>>> I haven't encountered this issue before. There were some problems in 
>>>> the past when another process hadn't finished writing a file yet that Warp 
>>>> would try to read, but that has been fixed. The file discovery thread 
>>>> shouldn't crash anymore when encountering a problem reading one of the 
>>>> files. What happens if you split the files in two folders, isolating the 
>>>> 1128 it doesn't see?
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>> Dimitry
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Sunday, July 15, 2018 at 8:33:21 PM UTC+2, mcia...@umich.edu wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>> Hi Dimitry, 
>>>>>
>>>>> We have noticed that the Warp directory being processed has ~2800 
>>>>> movies, but the total listing of movies in the "Overview" tab only 
>>>>> totals 1,672. Is there a way to force the program to double check for the 
>>>>> presence of additional files? I've tried stopping, exiting, restarting but 
>>>>> it comes back to the same number.  Have you seen this before?
>>>>>
>>>>> Mike
>>>>>
>>>>
System.IO.IOException: An unexpected network error occurred.

   at System.IO.__Error.WinIOError(Int32 errorCode, String maybeFullPath)
   at System.IO.FileStream.Init(String path, FileMode mode, FileAccess access, Int32 rights, Boolean useRights, FileShare share, Int32 bufferSize, FileOptions options, SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES secAttrs, String msgPath, Boolean bFromProxy, Boolean useLongPath, Boolean checkHost)
   at System.IO.FileStream..ctor(String path, FileMode mode, FileAccess access, FileShare share)
   at System.Xml.XmlTextWriter..ctor(String filename, Encoding encoding)
   at Warp.Movie.SaveMeta() in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Movie.cs:line 893
   at Warp.Movie.ExportMovie(Image originalStack, ProcessingOptionsMovieExport options) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Movie.cs:line 3190
   at WarpWorker.Program.Main(String[] args) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpWorker\Program.cs:line 180

System.AggregateException: One or more errors occurred. ---> System.IO.IOException: An unexpected network error occurred.

   at System.IO.__Error.WinIOError(Int32 errorCode, String maybeFullPath)
   at System.IO.FileStream.WriteCore(Byte[] buffer, Int32 offset, Int32 count)
   at Warp.Tools.IOHelper.WriteMapFloat(String path, MapHeader header, Single[][] data) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Tools\IOHelper.cs:line 388
   at Warp.Image.WriteMRC(String path, Single pixelSize, Boolean doStatistics, HeaderMRC header) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Image.cs:line 574
   at Warp.Movie.<>c__DisplayClass266_2.<ExportMovie>b__7() in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Movie.cs:line 3001
   at System.Threading.Tasks.Task.Execute()
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
   at System.Threading.Tasks.Task.ThrowIfExceptional(Boolean includeTaskCanceledExceptions)
   at System.Threading.Tasks.Task.Wait(Int32 millisecondsTimeout, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at System.Threading.Tasks.Task.Wait()
   at Warp.Movie.ExportMovie(Image originalStack, ProcessingOptionsMovieExport options) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Movie.cs:line 3183
   at WarpWorker.Program.Main(String[] args) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpWorker\Program.cs:line 180
---> (Inner Exception #0) System.IO.IOException: An unexpected network error occurred.

   at System.IO.__Error.WinIOError(Int32 errorCode, String maybeFullPath)
   at System.IO.FileStream.WriteCore(Byte[] buffer, Int32 offset, Int32 count)
   at Warp.Tools.IOHelper.WriteMapFloat(String path, MapHeader header, Single[][] data) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Tools\IOHelper.cs:line 388
   at Warp.Image.WriteMRC(String path, Single pixelSize, Boolean doStatistics, HeaderMRC header) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Image.cs:line 574
   at Warp.Movie.<>c__DisplayClass266_2.<ExportMovie>b__7() in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Movie.cs:line 3001
   at System.Threading.Tasks.Task.Execute()<---
